pi_items.source: Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 11) and export_result.sh (line 14). All task scripts use this identical path for the device identity CSV file.

pi_items.verified_value: ["Multiparameter Monitor", "Pulse Oximeter", "Infusion Pump"] - All three are confirmed as supported OpenICE simulated device types
pi_items.source: OpenICE documentation and research literature (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28734113/), README.md evidence_docs confirm Multiparameter Monitor at line 28, and icu_monitoring_setup README confirms all three device types are available options
Confirmed in task.json description (line 5) and verifier.py CSV parsing logic (line 63 checks for these exact field names)

pi_items.verified_value: Standard UUID v4 format: 8-4-4-4-12 hexadecimal digits separated by hyphens = 36 characters total (32 hex digits + 4 hyphens). Regex in export_result.sh line 32 confirms this pattern.
pi_items.source: UUID RFC 4122 standard verified through https://fastuuid.com/learn-about-uuids/uuid-36-characters and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universally_unique_identifier. Task regex pattern: [0-9a-fA-F]{8}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{4}-[0-9a-fA-F]{12}

pi_items.verified_value: OpenICE is a real, open-source medical device interoperability platform developed by the MD PnP (Medical Device Plug-and-Play) program at Massachusetts General Hospital
pi_items.source: https://www.openice.info/, https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28734113/ confirm OpenICE is an established platform for medical device simulation and interoperability

pi_items.verified_value: Data is synthetic - OpenICE creates simulated/virtual medical devices, not real patient data. Devices are generated programmatically with UUIDs extracted from OpenICE logs.
pi_items.source: Task description and setup_task.sh/export_result.sh confirm devices are simulated. export_result.sh extracts UUIDs from logs during task execution, confirming synthetic generation.

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified. The task requires agents to create exactly three simulated medical devices in OpenICE (Multiparameter Monitor, Pulse Oximeter, and Infusion Pump) and record their UUIDs in a CSV file at /home/ga/Desktop/device_identity_map.csv. UUIDs follow the standard 36-character format (8-4-4-4-12 hexadecimal pattern). All three device types are confirmed supported in OpenICE, an open-source interoperability platform by Harvard MD PnP. The data is entirely synthetic, generated during task execution from OpenICE simulation environment.
